name: Blueberry Apple Crisp
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup butter or margarine, softened to room temperature but not too soft
2 large tart apples or 3 medium apples, sliced, peeled, and cut into bite-sized pieces
enough blueberries to cover the bottom of the pan with one layer (I have never measured)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine all dry ingredients (flour, oats, pecans, and seasonings). Cut the butter into the dry ingredients using 2 knives in each hand, a pastry/dough blender, or a food processor for only a few short pulses until crumbly but barely sticky. Do not overblend. Set the topping aside.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease an 8x8 pan, 9x7 casserole dish, or comparable sized pan. Cover the bottom of the pan with one layer of blueberries (they can still be frozen). Cover the blueberries with apples. Sprinkle the topping over the fruit.
3. Bake for 20-35 minutes until the apples are easily pierced.
